title: Today's Tech Brief: Rehumanizing global health care with agentic AI
summary: A look at the intersection of technology and healthcare, with a focus on AI and its potential to rehumanize global healthcare.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Rehumanizing global health care with agentic AI
imageCaption: Rehumanizing global health care with agentic AI
imageSourceTitle: Rehumanizing global health care with agentic AI

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's Tech Brief. I'm excited to dive into some of the latest developments in the world of technology, particularly where it intersects with healthcare. We've got a couple of interesting stories to explore, and I'm looking forward to walking you through them.

First up, let's talk about the global healthcare sector. It's no secret that it's under a lot of strain. Years of underinvestment and recruitment challenges have coincided with a surge in demand for services, particularly for aging populations. But what if I told you that agentic AI might be a game-changer here? According to a piece in MIT Technology Review, AI could help rehumanize global healthcare by taking on some of the administrative burdens that currently plague the system. This could free up healthcare workers to focus on what they do best: caring for patients.

On a more security-focused note, it seems like Cisco's SD-WAN is in a bit of a pickle. There's another zero-day vulnerability under attack, and – and this is the kicker – there's no patch in sight. I feel for the sys admins out there; it's like trying to plug holes in a dam without any spare parts. According to The Register, this one's got people scratching their heads, wondering how to keep their networks safe without a fix on the horizon.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
